ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] Explore one of the nutrition books listed below or[ visit a farmer's market ](https://www.usdalocalfoodportal.com/fe/fdirectory_farmersmarket/?source=fe&directory=farmersmarket&location=&x=&y=)near you.
[ ] Write down a scripture, quote, or thought that reminds you why fitness is important to your family. Place it in a spot where you will see it frequently as a family.
[ ] Check out page 133 in the 303 Tween-Approved Exercises and Active Games book for outdoor activities you can do if the weather is warm where you live. The pool exercises are especially family-friendly.
[ ] (Historic Dancing) Gather friends and family and teach them the term's dances.

[ ] Make a healthy treat to share with neighbors or take one to the local fire or police station.
[ ] If you are not already certified, consider taking a first aid, CPR, lifeguarding, or babysitting course through the [Red Cross](https://www.redcross.org/take-a-class/cpr)
[ ] Organize a morning or afternoon of activities for younger children to give their parents a break. [This book](https://a.co/d/au799U8) has many ideas. (If you have young children at home, perhaps teen(s) can plan this to give their own parents a break!)
[ ] (Historic Dancing) Gather friends and family and teach them the term's dances.
[ ] Write down a scripture, quote, or thought that reminds you why fitness is important to your family. Place it in a spot where you will see it frequently as a family.
[ ] Check out page 133 in the 303 Tween-Approved Exercises and Active Games book for outdoor activities you can do if the weather is warm where you live. The pool exercises are especially family-friendly.

[ ] (Historic Dancing) Gather friends and family and teach them the term's dances.
[ ] Write down a scripture, quote, or thought that reminds you why fitness is important to your family. Place it in a spot where you will see it frequently as a family.
[ ] Check out page 133 in the 303 Tween-Approved Exercises and Active Games book for outdoor activities you can do if the weather is warm where you live. The pool exercises are especially family-friendly.
